finding: Official page states the rider can choose preferred cadence and the AI-based ECVT keeps it consistent, with smooth continuous adjustment, no gear steps unless desired, and optional manual shifting.
Official page positions X-system as motor plus transmission in one unit, fewer parts, reduced failure risk and maintenance-free transmission, plus connected security/service features through CCU.
Transition Velo describes X1/X1P as integrated eCVT motors at 120 Nm/1200 W and 150 Nm/1500 W respectively, aligned with Gobao's official two power blocks.
conditions_or_scope: Media article; official Gobao page does not expose X1/X1P names in extracted text

finding: Media explicitly identifies thermal management, long-term reliability, software responsiveness and energy efficiency as the real validation points for Gobao's eCVT route.
Media states Gobao had no partner or bike to show at that moment with the new motors.
Transition Velo reports MG Concept as Avinox's prototype integrating motor and electronic gearbox, developed with Canyon, Commencal, Forbidden, Mondraker and Megamo.
Media reports cadence-based auto mode, AI+ mode, simulated/customizable gears, around 520% range, shift under heavy load and at standstill, and under 0.1 s shift time.
conditions_or_scope: Media article; later reinforced by V032 booth-image quasi-production evidence

finding: Velobiz reports Avinox presented MG Concept at Eurobike and worked with Canyon, Commencal, Forbidden, Mondraker and Megamo; it also reports 19 partner brands at the Avinox stand for broader Avinox systems.

Velobiz reports Avinox's claimed benefits: no derailleur or multi-speed cassette with single-speed chain or belt, low-maintenance internal gearbox, adaptable platform and natural feel above 150 kg total moving mass.

BikeRumor reports Avinox had no official press kit available and would not confirm whether the MG Concept used a CVT inside, despite describing customizable steps and an infinitely variable transmission claim.
conditions_or_scope: Event media with booth-display observation; keep mechanism boundary visible

finding: BikeRumor reports booth/display-level figures: 520% range, <0.1 s shifts, shifting under load up to 300 Nm or at standstill, about 45 dB like M2S/M2, auto shift, straight chainline and multiple e-bike type suitability.
conditions_or_scope: Event/booth evidence; use as Avinox production-direction and quasi-production target information for internal benchmarking; final public datasheet remains a separate closure item

finding: Transition Velo reports Owuru Ride as a prototype motor with integrated continuous variation, 150 Nm max torque, 1000 W peak, 3.9 kg, 500% variation range and target-cadence operation.
Article states Owuru seeks partners for battery, display and controller to offer a complete system to OEMs; Rockrider was expected as first bike, with other OEM bikes hoped during 2027.
Review reports target-cadence no-shift operation between 40 and 90 rpm and describes the system as smooth, reactive and particularly suited to urban use.

Review notes weight, audible chain rollers, chain maintenance, speed-limit feel and battery reserve required for variation-control motor; these are lessons for e-CVT MGU packaging and UX, not direct MGU failure evidence.
Official enviolo evidence shows automatic preferred-cadence CVT behavior and enclosed low-upkeep hub route already solve many city/utility shifting pains without motor-internal integration.
Bosch eShift official evidence shows OEMs can integrate automatic/electronic shifting with multiple partner transmissions without choosing a motor-internal e-CVT MGU.

Tech Details six-image set should be treated as high-confidence Avinox production-direction and quasi-production product-definition evidence for internal benchmarking: torque smoothing, seamless shifting, configurable gear profiles, maintenance-free single-speed drivetrain, next-gen functions and six-platform fit.

Natural Ride Feel graphic defines value as torque smoothing and fast response with no overshoot, with assist remain/overrun behavior; it also claims reduced unsprung mass >1.1 kg, support heavier riders at 150 kg and 45 dB ultra-quiet operation.
conditions_or_scope: Booth image OCR; test basis still needs official closure

finding: Seamless Shifting graphic claims assist-off gear shift, stationary shifting, shift under load at 300 Nm, traditional shifting window >1 s, Avinox shifting window <0.1 s and constant torque output.
conditions_or_scope: Booth image OCR; torque measurement position and test conditions unknown

finding: Fully Customizable graphic states Avinox MG Drivetrain has infinitely variable gear ratio, configurable gear profiles, automatic/manual/multi-gear shifting and a 100%-520% range displayed across 1-13 virtual gears.
conditions_or_scope: Booth image OCR; internal mechanism not confirmed

finding: Maintenance-Free and Next-Gen Functions graphics claim legacy toolkit removal, single-speed style Avinox MG drivetrain, 3 times more durable, traditional drivetrain 10% less efficient, mechanical motor lock, electric reverse assist, built-in speed sensor and downhill charging graphic.
conditions_or_scope: Booth image OCR; durability/efficiency/charging basis needs validation

finding: Made For All Bike Platforms graphic explicitly covers eMTB, eTrekking, eSUV, eGravel, eCity and eCargo, supporting treatment as a cross-platform MGU product-definition signal rather than only an eMTB demo.
